title: Executive Order 12693—Exclusion of the Defense Mapping Agency Reston Center and Elements Under the Joint Special Operations Command From the Federal Labor-Management Relations Program

flags.justification: Exercises the same narrow, congressionally-authorized presidential determination mechanism (5 U.S.C. §7103(b)(1)) for specifically named national-security-related units, consistent with EO 12632.
The required statutory determinations are explicitly stated in the order's own text.
Single purpose covering two related organizational units.
The exclusion criteria are statutory, and the specific units are precisely named.
No incentive structure rewarding behavior contrary to the order's purpose is identifiable.
A standing exclusion determination for security-sensitive positions, evergreen by nature.
This exercises a specific, congressionally-anticipated exception mechanism rather than an ad hoc removal of oversight.
The underlying statutory mechanism anticipates judicial review of the specific application, as demonstrated by the AFGE litigation underlying EO 12632.
A narrow, specific-unit exclusion determination, a routine application of an already-established mechanism.
No second agency's competing mandate is implicated.
The exclusion is narrow (two specific units) and grounded in specific statutory criteria, not a broad undermining carve-out.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/16 = 0.0%. The third instance of this specific type of national-security labor-relations exclusion order found in this corpus (see EO 12171, EO 12632).
